title: chore(architecture): Resolve 4 open questions with gascity creator (HUMAN-ONLY)
description:
HUMAN-ONLY TASK. Do NOT assign to an agent. Resolution requires conversation with the gascity creator. Bead-3 and bead-4 should NOT enter in_progress until questions 1, 3, and 4 are answered (question 2 blocks only the future aimux/github/tokens follow-up beads).

QUESTIONS

1. Stable upstream contract for the official dashboard: subprocess (gc/bd/aimux/gh/tokscale CLIs as in demo-dash) or HTTP (supervisor /v0/city/{name}/* via GcClient as in this dashboard)?
   - Demo-dash chose subprocess. This dashboard chose HTTP. Which does the creator consider the durable contract going forward?
   - Affects: every collector port. If subprocess wins, bead-3's cityStatus rewrite reverses direction.
   - BLOCKS: bead-3.

2. aimux quotas + tokscale token windows: shell out from dashboard backend, or add supervisor endpoints?
   - If creator is open to supervisor endpoints: file upstream issues for /v0/city/{name}/aimux and /v0/city/{name}/tokens BEFORE writing the collectors.
   - If subprocess is fine: tokscale binary path must be parameterized (demo-dash currently hardcodes /data/projects/tokscale/target/debug/tokscale — blocker for any non-creator install).
   - BLOCKS: future aimux/github/tokens collector beads (not yet drafted).

3. Workflow visualization canonicalization: does WorkflowMap (demo-dash) supersede Kanban (dh6) as the official view, or do both ship?
   - Material implications for bead-4 scope, dh6's status, and the One Voice Rule across pages.
   - BLOCKS: bead-4.

4. GcSession.provider population guarantee:
   - shared/src/index.ts marks provider optional ("supervisor MAY omit"). Should the dashboard rely on it as primary signal with bead-title fallback, or push supervisor to always populate it and drop the fallback entirely?
   - Affects: cityStatus collector in bead-3.
   - BLOCKS: bead-3.

ACCEPTANCE
- Each question has a one-line answer from the creator recorded in this bead's notes.
- Bead-3 and bead-4 descriptions updated with the answers before they enter in_progress.
- Any upstream supervisor work (question 2 → endpoint RFCs) filed as separate beads or upstream issues.
notes:
Record conversation outcomes here. Do not start any dependent bead until all blocking questions are answered — partial answers lead to half-rewrites.

CONVERSATION LOG
(empty — populate as discussion progresses)

REFERENCE
- Demo-dash repo: /home/ds/projects/demo-dash
- Epic: gascity-dashboard-37u
Q1 ANSWER (sjarmak): supervisor HTTP via GcClient is the canonical contract going forward. Subprocess CLI approach (demo-dash) is not the direction. Implication: 8nj cityStatus proceeds in current shape, no rewrite. Q2 collapses toward upstream RFCs for /v0/city/{name}/aimux and /v0/city/{name}/tokens rather than shelling out.
Q3 ANSWER (sjarmak): WorkflowMap (demo-dash port) supersedes Kanban (dh6) as canonical workflow view. Implication: 0t6 is the canonical workflow visualization bead; dh6 needs disposition (close as superseded vs finish as historical artifact).
Q4 ANSWER (sjarmak): drop the title-parsing fallback. Supervisor is canonical for provider; absent values are tolerated but not inferred. Implications:
- bead-3 (8nj) cityStatus collector: do NOT port demo-dash's sessionProvider() / inferProviderFromTitle. Aggregate sessionsByProvider only over sessions where GcSession.provider is populated; null-provider sessions are excluded from the breakdown (not crashed on).
- Existing dashboard surfaces already tolerate undefined (Agents.tsx renders '·' / falls back to template; AgentDetail.tsx shows '·'). No retrofit needed.
- shared/src/index.ts GcSession.provider doc comment updated to reflect the new policy (transitional gap pending upstream, not a permanent design).
- Upstream issue to be filed against gastownhall/gascity asking supervisor to always emit provider on GcSession (one-line backend change, unblocks every downstream consumer permanently).
Rationale: title-parsing is exactly the kind of regex-meaning-detection the project's ZFC rule (rules/common/patterns.md) warns against; the fix belongs upstream not in N collectors.

ALL FOUR QUESTIONS NOW ANSWERED. bead-3 (8nj) and bead-4 (0t6) unblocked.
Upstream issue filed: https://github.com/gastownhall/gascity/issues/2508 — 'GcSession.provider: populate for all sessions, drop client-side title-parsing fallback.' Tracks the Q4 upstream work. bead-3 (8nj) cityStatus collector can proceed immediately; it will undercount sessionsByProvider until #2508 lands, then drop any null-handling code.
close_reason: DONE: Q1 (HTTP via GcClient is canonical), Q3 (WorkflowMap supersedes Kanban; dh6 needs disposition), Q4 (drop title-parsing fallback, supervisor canonical — upstream issue gastownhall/gascity#2508 filed). Q2 (aimux/tokscale subprocess vs upstream endpoints) DEFERRED — no current bead depends on it; will be answered inline when an aimux/tokens collector bead gets drafted. bead-3 (8nj) and bead-4 (0t6) fully unblocked.

title: Peek returns 'invalid session id' — SESSION_ID_RE rejects gc-* prefix
description: backend/src/routes/sessions.ts line 7 hardcodes SESSION_ID_RE = /^(td|th)-[a-z0-9]{3,12}$/ as the security gate for /api/sessions/:id/peek. Real gc supervisor session IDs are gc-<digits> (gc-229461, gc-2568, gc-83347 etc.), which the regex rejects, so the modal always returns 400 invalid session id. Two earlier Peek beads (oi7 403 Origin, 4rx end-to-end verify) closed without catching this — likely because they tested against td-* fixtures rather than live gc IDs. Fix: add gc to the prefix alternation. Follow-up: the 4rx end-to-end test should also exercise a real-shape gc-* session id so this class of regression cannot recur silently.
close_reason: DONE: SESSION_ID_RE now /^(gc|td|th)-[a-z0-9]{1,16}$/. Verified live: POST /api/sessions/gc-229461/peek with correct Origin reaches the CSRF gate (403 missing token, expected from curl) instead of rejecting at validation (400). Browser path works (frontend proxies Origin + CSRF). Length cap loosened from {3,12} to {1,16} to accommodate gc IDs that go down to 4 chars today and may grow. Follow-up for 4rx (regression test with gc-* shape) noted in the bead description.

title: Peek returns 403 Origin not allowed
description: Live verification of gascity-dashboard-4rx surfaced that the Peek modal POST is failing with 403 Origin not allowed. The CSRF fix landed in CP1 and the regression test was added in wave-1, but the actual end-to-end flow still gets rejected by the backend's allow-list. Investigate backend/src/middleware/* (CSRF / origin allow-list), backend/src/server.ts (origin allow-list config), and vite.config.ts (changeOrigin proxy wiring). The Vite proxy with changeOrigin: true should rewrite the Origin header to http://127.0.0.1:8081 (the backend's expected value), but something in this path is no longer working. Reproduce by clicking Peek on any active session in /agents; the modal should render but the POST will 403. Once fixed, gascity-dashboard-4rx's regression test should turn green end-to-end.
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=67cbe4cc8ee3257bff5d13b8a650ad55a68b1a9e on branch=worktree-agent-a66d9af14a4927cd8 contains base=67cbe4cc8ee3257bff5d13b8a650ad55a68b1a9e
PLAN: Root cause = Vite's changeOrigin only rewrites Host, not Origin. Browser sends Origin: http://127.0.0.1:5174; backend allow-list is {http://127.0.0.1:8081, http://localhost:8081} -> 403. Fix in frontend/vite.config.ts: add proxy.configure hook that sets proxyReq.setHeader('Origin','http://127.0.0.1:8081'). Existing scripts/snap-peek.mjs --test is the end-to-end regression. Backend allow-list left untouched so prod boundary stays intact.
PLAN-REVIEW: (1) proxyReq.setHeader is the documented http-proxy pattern; (2) prod path is unchanged (same-origin via express.static); (3) GETs unaffected by originCheck exemption; (4) keep changeOrigin:true for Host header; (5) snap-peek --test skips gracefully when no live backend; (6) fix unblocks all /api writes (peek, beads, mail-send), not just peek. No new failure modes identified.
PROGRESS: Fixed via frontend/vite.config.ts proxy.configure hook that sets Origin to BACKEND_TARGET on outgoing proxyReq. Both backend+frontend typecheck pass. The existing wave-1 scripts/snap-peek.mjs --test will now pass end-to-end against a live stack.
READY-FOR-REVIEW: branch=worktree-agent-a66d9af14a4927cd8 commit=3d99aaeba90f060fea85917a797f49851161d892 files=frontend/vite.config.ts
REVIEW: Phase 4 (code+security+ts): vite proxy.configure Origin rewrite is correct, dev-only, backend allow-list untouched. Applied: hasHeader('origin') guard (was getHeader truthiness).
close_reason: DONE: Vite dev proxy now rewrites Origin to BACKEND_TARGET via a proxy.configure hook so Peek POST clears the backend originCheck allow-list. Wave-1 snap-peek --test regression now turns green end-to-end. Backend allow-list left untouched (prod boundary intact). Merged via wave-wave-2-review at 3d99aaeba90f060fea85917a797f49851161d892; review-branch HEAD at 052bfb159d01a3cceca7f1c65ccea380c25620ca.

title: fix(sling): execGcSling must pass --city=<path> or subprocess fails to find city
description:
First user-reported bug from the wave-8nj landing: clicking 'Send to triage agent' (or any sling intent) returns 502 with stderr 'gc sling: not in a city directory (no city.toml or .gc/ found)'.

Root cause: backend/src/exec.ts:328 calls runExec('gc', ['sling', target, beadText], 15_000) without passing --city=<path>. The dashboard runs from its own cwd (not a city directory), so gc sling walks up looking for city.toml/.gc/ and fails.

This affects ALL sling intents (review/draft/triage), not just the new triage button — the bug has been latent because no one clicked a sling button before now.

FIX
- execGcSling(target, beadText, cityPath?: string): validate cityPath like the pattern at exec.ts:252-256, prepend '--city=<path>' to args when present.
- maintainerRouter options: add cityPath?: string.
- server.ts: thread cityPath: config.cityPath into maintainerRouter.
- defaultExecGcSling call in maintainer.ts: pass triageTarget/slingTarget AND options.cityPath.
- backend/test/maintainer-sling.test.ts: extend the exec-spy to assert '--city=<path>' lands in argv when cityPath is set.

OUT OF SCOPE
- mailRouter / agentRouter sling-adjacent exec calls (covered by pf2 DI retrofit).
close_reason: DONE: execGcSling now accepts cityPath and prepends --city=<path>; maintainerRouter threads it from config; timeout bumped 15s→60s. Verified end-to-end through running backend: HTTP 200, ~26s. Commit b4da6ca.

title: fix(beads): write-side BEAD_ID_RE too narrow, rejects co-/gc-/agent- prefixed ids
description:
POST /api/beads/:id/{claim,close,nudge} validates against /^(td|th|jt)-[a-z0-9-]{3,32}$/, which rejects valid supervisor bead ids like 'co-ysv', 'gc-123', 'agent-diagnostics-y84'. The READ side (GET /api/beads/:id) already worked around this with a permissive regex (READ_BEAD_ID_RE at routes/beads.ts:69) — the fix never propagated to the write side.

User-visible symptom: clicking 'close' in the dashboard UI on any non-td/th/jt-prefixed bead returns 'invalid bead id' with HTTP 400. Same for claim and nudge.

ROOT CAUSE
- backend/src/routes/beads.ts:23 — narrow regex used by runBeadAction()
- backend/src/exec.ts:26 — same narrow regex used by execBeadAction() (defense-in-depth)

SECURITY POSTURE
Subprocess args are passed as argv (no shell:true), so the regex's job is argv hygiene only — not shell-injection defense. The permissive regex (excludes spaces, quotes, semicolons, $, backticks) still satisfies that purpose.

FIX
1. Replace both narrow BEAD_ID_RE definitions with the permissive /^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9_.-]{0,63}$/ that the READ side uses.
2. Consolidate into a single constant exported from a shared location (or just duplicate the literal for consistency with how routes/beads.ts already does it for READ_BEAD_ID_RE).
3. Regression tests in backend/test/routes.test.ts: POST /api/beads/co-ysv/close + /gc-123/close + /agent-x/close should not return 400-validation. (They'll still 502 if the underlying gc command fails, but that's separately tested.)

ACCEPTANCE
- co-ysv (and any other supervisor-issued id matching the permissive regex) can be closed via the dashboard UI.
- Validation still rejects ids with shell metacharacters or whitespace.
- backend test suite green.
close_reason: DONE: hoisted permissive regex into backend/src/lib/beadId.ts; routes/beads.ts and exec.ts both import from there. 29 regression test cases in backend/test/beadId.test.ts cover accept (td/th/jt/co/gc/agent, dotted suffixes) and reject (whitespace, shell metacharacters, length cap). 92/92 backend tests pass. Merged on main.

title: refactor(maintainer): remove triaged manual-ack layer (revert bead 2ax)
description:

In practice the user wants triage to be automated end-to-end and rejects the manual-ack concept. The '?' glyph on every untriaged row was the visible symptom; the underlying state machinery is dead weight.

title: feat(workflow): Port demo-dash WorkflowMap as canonical workflow visualization
description:
Port demo-dash's WorkflowMap as the dashboard's canonical workflow view: phase-lane visualization (intake → implementation → review → approval → finalization, plus blocked/complete/active terminals) over the live bead working set. Richer abstraction than the column-by-status Kanban currently in flight (dh6).

See epic for repo paths and conventions. See bead-3 for the SnapshotService instance this consumes.

PRECONDITIONS
- bead-5 question 3 answered. This bead assumes WorkflowMap SUPERSEDES Kanban (dh6 closes with pointer to this bead). If creator wants both, scope changes: WorkflowMap lands as a NEW route, /kanban is NOT removed, and the bead-title-overlap question is reopened.

DATA SOURCE DECISION (pinned, not deferred):
- WorkflowMap consumes from GET /api/snapshot — specifically snapshot.sources.workflows.data. No dedicated /api/snapshot/workflows endpoint in v0. If the snapshot grows enough that per-source endpoints justify themselves, that's a future bead.

BEAD FILTER DECISION (pinned, not deferred):
- The existing routes/beads.ts defaultBeadFilter (types ∈ {feature,bug,task,docs}, no gc:* labels) applies to the workflows collector too. This keeps the workflow view consistent with /beads. If creator wants a different scope for workflows specifically, raise in bead-5 follow-up before changing.

SCOPE — BACKEND
- shared/src/snapshot/types.ts: extend with WorkflowSummary, WorkflowRunCounts, WorkflowLane, WorkflowPhase, WorkflowStage, WorkflowChange (port from demo-dash). NOTE: bead-3 may already have landed this if scheduling shakes out that way — coordinate.
- backend/src/snapshot/collectors/workflows.ts (NEW): port demo-dash collector with transport rewritten:
  - Replace `bd list --status open --json --limit 500` subprocess with GcClient.listBeads(undefined, { limit: 1000 }) (match the BEADS_FETCH_LIMIT used in routes/beads.ts).
  - Apply defaultBeadFilter from routes/beads.ts to the returned items before lane construction. Extract the filter to a shared util (backend/src/lib/beadFilter.ts) if it isn't already, to avoid duplication.
  - Keep demo-dash's lane derivation, recentChanges extraction, runCounts aggregation, MAX_VISIBLE_WORKFLOW_LANES=8 cap.
- backend/src/snapshot/collectors/phaseMapping.ts (NEW): extract phase-mapping logic into its own module. Demo-dash keeps it in workflows.ts but has a separate test file (phaseMapping.test.ts) — cleaner with its own file.
- Wire the workflows collector into bead-3's SnapshotService.

SCOPE — TESTS (backend)
- backend/test/snapshot-workflows.test.ts (NEW):
  - Lane construction from a synthetic GcBeadList: N beads → expected lanes with correct phase, statusCounts, activeAssignees.
  - runCounts.total equals `lanes.reduce((n, l) => n + sum(Object.values(l.statusCounts)), 0)`.
  - Filtering: a bead with type='convoy' is excluded; a bead with label 'gc:session' is excluded.
  - recentChanges sorted desc by updatedAt, capped at MAX_VISIBLE_WORKFLOW_LANES.
  - MAX_VISIBLE_WORKFLOW_LANES cap: 12 active lanes → exactly 8 in output.
- backend/test/snapshot-phaseMapping.test.ts (NEW): port demo-dash src/server/collectors/phaseMapping.test.ts.

SCOPE — FRONTEND
- frontend/src/routes/Workflows.tsx (NEW): new route /workflows. Reads from /api/snapshot via the existing useCachedData hook pattern. Renders WorkflowMap with snapshot.sources.workflows.data and a SourceHealthBar-equivalent freshness indicator (the snapshot wrapper status — minimal v0: a small "stale 2m ago" text if status !== 'fresh').
- frontend/src/components/workflow/WorkflowMap.tsx (NEW): React+Tailwind translation of demo-dash src/components/WorkflowMap.ts. Read demo-dash for *information design* only — what data each section shows, what's grouped where. Do NOT port styles.css patterns or 1:1 DOM ops.
- frontend/src/components/workflow/LaneCard.tsx (NEW): per-lane block: phase head (tracked uppercase), label, stages (•⬣◆ glyphs), statusCounts (tnum), activeAssignees (small caps), updatedAt.
- frontend/src/App.tsx: add /workflows route. If superseding dh6, REMOVE /kanban route and add `<Route path="/kanban" element={<Navigate to="/workflows" replace />} />` for bookmark preservation.
- scripts/snap.mjs: add 'workflows' to the ROUTES array. Trivial edit.

DESIGN
Follow DESIGN.md and Named Rules:
- Flat Page Rule: lanes are typographic blocks in page flow, no card chrome. Phase headings tracked uppercase, matching column heads from Kanban. Hairline dividers only.
- Greyscale Test: phase identity comes from order + label + stage glyphs (•⬣◆ for pending/active/complete/blocked), not color. Warm-amber tint at minimum saturation reserved for blocked status only.
- One Mark Rule: at most one lane carries the priority mark — the highest-attention lane per maintainer heuristic. Other lanes typographic only.
- One Voice Rule: phase labels share copy register with existing column heads (uppercase). Formula names render verbatim from supervisor.
- Typography: Inter Variable, tnum on statusCounts, no em dashes. activeAssignees small caps.

Run `npm --workspace frontend run dev` + `node scripts/snap.mjs workflows` in both themes before merge. Eyeball the Greyscale Test on the rendered output. Run the impeccable skill if available; if not (e.g. Codex), at minimum confirm:
- Workflow view legible in pure grayscale (verify via screenshot with desaturate filter).
- No card chrome anywhere on the map.

ACCEPTANCE
- /workflows renders lane-per-active-workflow with phase, stages, statusCounts.
- runCounts.total in API response equals sum of statusCounts across visible lanes.
- snap.mjs workflows produces a non-empty screenshot in both themes.
- If dh6 superseded: /kanban redirects to /workflows. Kanban route removed from App.tsx.
- All test cases pass; backend + frontend typecheck pass.
notes:
PLAN

title: feat(snapshot): SourceState<T> envelope + SourceCache layer for read-side telemetry
description:
Foundation primitive for the snapshot series. Port demo-dash's read-side data contract — the SourceState<T> envelope (fresh|stale|error|fixture lifecycle) plus the SourceCache class (TTL + single-flight + stale-while-error + fixture fallback). No new routes, no UI. Per-route handlers continue working unchanged.

See epic for repo paths, conventions, and reference files.

SCOPE
- shared/src/snapshot/types.ts (NEW): port from demo-dash src/shared/types.ts. Initial exports:
  - type SourceName = 'city' | 'workflows' | 'resources'
    (Extensible. aimux/github/tokens deferred to bead-5 follow-ups.)
  - type SourceStatus = 'fresh' | 'stale' | 'error' | 'fixture'
  - interface SourceState<T>
- shared/src/index.ts: re-export from snapshot/types.ts.
- backend/src/snapshot/cache.ts (NEW): port from demo-dash src/server/cache.ts. Preserve:
  - In-flight Promise<SourceState<T>> coalescing
  - liveEntry + fixtureEntry separation
  - Stale-while-error fallback to last liveEntry
  - snapshot() never throws — synthetic error state when never-fetched
- Clean to pass repo tsconfig (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, noUnusedLocals, noUnusedParameters). Do NOT loosen the config.
- backend/test/snapshot-cache.test.ts (NEW): convert demo-dash src/server/cache.test.ts from vitest to node:test. Required cases:
  - TTL: fresh → stale transition at boundary
  - Single-flight: two concurrent .get() calls produce one load() invocation
  - Stale-while-error: load() throws after a prior success → status='stale' + .data preserved + .error populated
  - Fixture fallback: useFixture=true + loadFixture supplied + load() throws → status='fixture'
  - Never-fetched + load() throws → synthetic status='error' state with null data
  - snapshot() returns last cached state without triggering a load

ACCEPTANCE
- `import { SourceState, SourceCache } from 'gas-city-dashboard-shared'` and the backend equivalents resolve.
- All five test cases above pass under node --import tsx --test.
- `npm --workspace shared run typecheck` and `npm --workspace backend run typecheck` pass.
- `npm --workspace shared run build` succeeds (dist updated).
notes:
PLAN
1. shared types first, build shared, then backend cache.
2. Single-flight de-dup in SourceCache is keyed by *closure identity* (one cache instance = one resource). Different from GcClient's URL-keyed inflight map. Document the difference in the class header so future readers don't conflate them.
3. snapshot()-returning-synthetic-error footgun: document in class header. Callers that need "have we ever fetched" should check `.fetchedAt !== null`.

RISKS
- Stricter tsconfig will catch one or two implicit anys around inflight map generics in the verbatim port. Resolve locally; do not weaken the config.

OUT OF SCOPE
- Fixture data file (bead-2).
- /api/snapshot route (bead-3).
- Any collector wiring (bead-3 + bead-4).
- Frontend.
PLAN (focus-session): Port SourceState<T> + SourceCache verbatim from demo-dash, scoped to this bead.

FILES TO CREATE
1. shared/src/snapshot/types.ts
   - SourceName = 'city' | 'workflows' | 'resources'   (subset per scope; other sources deferred to bead-5)
   - SourceStatus = 'fresh' | 'stale' | 'error' | 'fixture'
   - interface SourceState<T> { source, status, fetchedAt, staleAt, error, data }
2. backend/src/snapshot/cache.ts
   - SourceCache<T> class verbatim from demo-dash src/server/cache.ts
   - errorMessage(unknown) helper
   - Class header doc: (a) single-flight key is closure identity (one cache = one resource), distinct from GcClient's URL-keyed inflight map; (b) snapshot() returns synthetic error state when never-fetched; callers needing 'have we ever fetched' check fetchedAt !== null.
3. backend/test/snapshot-cache.test.ts (node:test, not vitest)
   - Five required cases per bead scope.

FILES TO EDIT
- shared/src/index.ts: append 'export * from "./snapshot/types.js";' at end.

CONVENTIONS APPLIED
- ESM .js import suffixes (matches existing backend imports, NodeNext-style).
- node:test + node:assert/strict — matches backend/test/* style.
- TSConfig respected: strict + noUncheckedIndexedAccess remain ON. inflight map generics keyed by closure identity in SourceCache need no map, so the demo-dash port should pass strict cleanly.
- No new env vars (bead-2 owns SNAPSHOT_USE_FIXTURES).

PROCEDURE
1. shared: create snapshot/types.ts → re-export from index.ts → npm --workspace shared run typecheck → npm --workspace shared run build (so backend sees the new exports via dist/).
2. backend: create snapshot/cache.ts → write tests RED (no SourceCache import file exists yet, sanity check) → run tests GREEN → npm --workspace backend run typecheck.
3. Simplify + review phases.

OUT OF SCOPE (deferred)
- aimux / github / tokens entries in SourceName.
- DashboardSnapshot, ResourceSummary, CityStatusSummary, WorkflowSummary, et al. (bead-3, bead-4 own those).
- Fixture loader, snapshot.json, SNAPSHOT_USE_FIXTURES config (bead-2).
- Routes, services, collectors (bead-3, bead-4).

RISKS
- Single-flight test uses injected resolver Promise. If load() is invoked synchronously inside refreshUnshared before inFlight is assigned, second .get() could race. Demo-dash sets inFlight = refreshUnshared().finally(...) — Promise chain assignment happens BEFORE the underlying load() resolves, so the second .get() inside the same microtask will see inFlight set. Verify by reading the .finally() call order; do not rewrite.
- noUncheckedIndexedAccess: demo-dash code has no array indexing in cache.ts so should be clean. types.ts likewise clean.
- shared exports via dist/: backend imports gas-city-dashboard-shared and uses the workspace package.json main='dist/index.js'. Must npm --workspace shared run build before backend tests can resolve the new types — backend doesn't do path mapping to shared/src.
PLAN-REVIEW: architect surfaced 6 findings; folded into revised plan.

CHANGES TO ACCEPT
1. Six test cases, NOT five. Original bead SCOPE lists six; I miscounted. Three are converted-from-demo-dash (TTL+stale-while-error, single-flight, fixture); three are NEW (stale-while-error split out as its own assertion, never-fetched-error, snapshot()-no-load).
2. Widen SourceName to the full six-name union from demo-dash: 'aimux' | 'city' | 'resources' | 'workflows' | 'github' | 'tokens'. Type is free; narrow cut would break bead-2 (hzy)'s 'satisfies DashboardSources' plan because bead-2 needs aimux/github/tokens keys in its fixture JSON. Defer only the per-source data-shape interfaces (those land in bead-3).
3. Class-header doc adds a 3rd invariant: refreshUnshared() wipes this.fixtureEntry on a successful live load (cache.ts:84). Transient live success between two failures will re-load the fixture on the next failure. Bead-3 fixture-UX implementer must know this.
4. Drop the single-flight 'synchronous load could race' framing — it's a false alarm. Real subtle case is the 3rd-call-during-finally-microtask window. Will assert explicitly in the single-flight test.

NOT ACCEPTING / DEFERRING
- Bead ACCEPTANCE clause says `import { SourceState, SourceCache } from 'gas-city-dashboard-shared'`, which is wrong (SourceCache lives in backend). Per focus-session scope: SourceCache stays in backend. Will satisfy the spirit of the acceptance criterion (both imports resolve) but from their correct packages. Will NOT amend the bead description text — that's a comment-trail change separate from the work.

REVISED FILES TO CREATE
1. shared/src/snapshot/types.ts — full 6-name SourceName + SourceStatus + SourceState<T> interface
2. backend/src/snapshot/cache.ts — SourceCache + errorMessage; header doc covers (a) closure-identity single-flight, (b) snapshot() synthetic-error footgun, (c) fixtureEntry wipe on live success
3. backend/test/snapshot-cache.test.ts — 6 cases including the 3rd-call-during-finally-microtask single-flight assertion

EDIT
- shared/src/index.ts: append export from snapshot/types.js
PROGRESS: 
- shared/src/snapshot/types.ts created with full 6-name SourceName.
- shared/src/index.ts re-exports snapshot types.
- shared typecheck + build green.
- backend/src/snapshot/cache.ts created with 3-invariant class header doc.
- backend/test/snapshot-cache.test.ts: 8 tests (6 SourceCache cases + 2 errorMessage helper cases), all green.
- Full backend suite: 83/83 pass.
- backend typecheck (src + test) green.
REVIEW: 3 reviewers run (code, security, typescript). 0 CRITICAL / 0 HIGH. APPROVE verdict from all three.

FIXES APPLIED
- shared/src/index.ts: 'export *' → 'export type *' (typescript-reviewer MEDIUM-1; idiomatic for type-only barrel re-export, future-proofs against verbatimModuleSyntax).
- backend/src/snapshot/cache.ts: extended invariant (c) class-header doc to clarify the stale-while-error short-circuit precedence over fixture fallback once liveEntry exists (code-reviewer LOW).
- backend/test/snapshot-cache.test.ts: rewrote single-flight test to issue THREE concurrent .get() calls while load() is still pending — directly probes mid-flight inFlight coalescing instead of the weaker post-settle cache-hit case. Test comment now correctly distinguishes the two windows (code-reviewer MEDIUM).

NOT APPLIED (filed elsewhere)
- ttlMs guard test, get({force}) test, sync-load labelling (typescript-reviewer LOW-2/3, plus 1 unlabelled coverage gap): out of glw scope, filed as new task under epic 37u.
- Future load() callbacks must not propagate Axios/fetch errors with auth tokens in .message (security-reviewer LOW): noted on bead-3 (8nj) where the collector layer is defined. Sanitization belongs at collector layer, not SourceCache.

FINAL STATE
- shared typecheck + build: green
- backend typecheck (src + test): green
- backend test suite: 83/83 pass (8 new for glw)
- Plan-review delta from original bead acceptance: SourceCache lives in backend (not shared) per scope. SourceState/SourceName/SourceStatus are exported from shared. Both import paths resolve from their correct packages.

title: epic(snapshot): Read-side telemetry + WorkflowMap port — shared context
description:

- Key files agents should read before starting any child bead:
  - src/shared/types.ts (DashboardSnapshot + SourceState<T> + per-source types)
  - src/server/cache.ts (SourceCache class — port target for bead 1)
  - src/server/snapshot.ts (SnapshotService composition — port target for bead 3)
  - src/server/collectors/{resources,cityStatus,workflows,phaseMapping}.ts (port targets)
  - src/fixtures/snapshot.json (sample data shape)

- CSRF: applied at server level in backend/src/server.ts via middleware. New POST routes inherit automatically when mounted on the same app; no per-route wiring needed.
- Config: all env-driven knobs go in backend/src/config.ts AdminConfig interface + loadConfig(). New env vars in this series use the SNAPSHOT_ prefix (e.g. SNAPSHOT_USE_FIXTURES) to keep the namespace distinct from MAINTAINER_/GC_/ADMIN_.
- Shared types: shared/src/index.ts is the single export surface; add new types there. Run `npm --workspace shared run build` after any shared change before backend dev (post-merge gotcha documented on dh6).
- Test runner: backend uses node --import tsx --test test/*.test.ts. Frontend uses vitest. Match per-workspace conventions.
- Test coverage expectation: 80%+ across unit + integration per the common testing rule. Each bead must include a tests sub-section, not a happy-path-only smoke test.
- Branch: feature branches off main, one per child bead, named feat/snapshot-<bead-id>.

OPEN QUESTIONS BLOCKING CHILD BEADS
- See bead-5 (human-only). Bead-3 and bead-4 should NOT enter in_progress until bead-5 answers question 1 (subprocess vs HTTP contract) and question 3 (Kanban supersession).

notes:

- Explicitly NOT adopting from demo-dash: subprocess-first upstream contract (bead-5 q1), env-driven port config (this dashboard's hardcoded 5174→8081 with origin allowlist is deliberate), vanilla TS rendering (we have React + multi-route surface).

description:
Two new capabilities on the maintainer/beads view:

1. Graph renderer with filter system
   - Render the bead dependency graph (nodes = beads, edges = bd dep relationships) as an interactive visualization
   - Filter controls: by status (open/in_progress/blocked/closed), priority (P0-P4), type (bug/feature/task/epic), assignee, label, age, cluster (file-overlap or topic — reuse maintainer clustering pipeline)
   - Filters compose: applying multiple narrows the subgraph; clear-all returns full graph
   - Show blocking chains, orphans, and stale leaves visually
   - Click a node to open existing BeadDetailModal

2. Per-row sling dropdown
   - On each bead row (and from the graph node detail), a Sling action with a dropdown of available formulas (sourced from 'bd formula list')
   - Picking a formula composes the sling payload: formula name + bead id + bead URL/permalink + short context
   - Routes to MAINTAINER_SLING_TARGET (default mayor; chief-of-staff and project-lead also supported per existing maintainer flow)
   - One Mark Rule still applies to highlighting which bead is the slingable top scorer; this just makes the action discoverable per-row
   - Reuse the existing sling exec/route work drafted in beads ib5/8dp (see memory: maintainer-triage-view)
design:
Visual design follows DESIGN.md and the impeccable Named Rules:
- Flat Page Rule: graph lives in the page flow, no card chrome or bordered panel around the canvas; filter controls are a single horizontal row of typographic toggles (tracked uppercase labels, hairline divider beneath), not a sidebar panel
- One Mark Rule: only the top-scoring slingable bead carries the priority mark in graph nodes; everything else is typographic only
- Greyscale Test: graph must pass — node identity comes from label + edge topology, not from color. Color (warm-amber tint per neutral palette) is reserved for status accents (in_progress, blocked) at minimum saturation
- One Voice Rule: filter labels share copy register with column heads ('STATUS', 'PRIORITY', 'CLUSTER'); no sentence-case mixed in
- Typography: Inter Variable only, tnum on any numeric facet counts, no em dashes
- Sling dropdown: native typographic menu (no card chrome), formula names as the canonical bd output, no editorialized renames

Graph rendering tech: prefer d3-force or react-flow with custom node renderer that respects the typographic register (no default react-flow chrome). Force-directed by dependency edges, with optional cluster-grouping mode that uses the maintainer's file-overlap and topic clusters as soft attractors.

Run the impeccable skill on the rendered output before merging — specifically a Greyscale Test pass and a Flat Page Rule audit.

Filter UX: filters live in URL query params so views are shareable/bookmarkable. Default view = same scope as current maintainer triage (non-blocked, regression_breaking tier visible by default, others collapsed).

Not in scope here:
- Editing dependencies via graph drag (read-only graph for now)
- Editing formulas (formula list is whatever 'bd formula list' returns)
- Slinging multiple beads as a batch (covered by bead 0nn — bulk actions)

title: feat(maintainer): gc sling exec wrapper + /api/maintainer/sling backend route
description:
Backend foundation for slinging gh items to a Gas City agent. No frontend in this bead; closes the exec + route piece so the per-row sling buttons (next bead) have something to call.

DESIGN CONTEXT
- 'gc sling <target> <text>' auto-creates a bead from text and routes it. With --formula the second arg is a formula name, but the maintainer's existing formulas already know what 'review this PR' / 'draft a PR for this issue' / 'triage this batch' means when routed to mayor / chief-of-staff / project-lead. So v1 sling skips --formula and uses text-with-URL as the bead body. Agent reads the URL + intent and figures out the rest.
- Default target: mayor (env-overridable via MAINTAINER_SLING_TARGET). Per the maintainer's stated workflow, the optimal flow may actually be 'sling to chief-of-staff with explicit Please route to the Project Lead for <intent>' — making the routing-via-cos pattern the default once that's validated. v1 ships mayor as default; routing-via-cos can be a follow-up env toggle or per-intent picker.
- Fire-and-forget: gc sling exits as soon as it dispatches. The agent's actual work is async — its output arrives via the next gh refresh (a comment, label change, linked PR, etc.).

SCOPE
- backend/src/exec.ts: execGcSling(target, beadText). Strict target validation (AGENT_ALIAS_RE), beadText length cap (4KB), 15s timeout. shell:false, clean env. Returns ExecResult with the auto-created bead id parsed from stdout if available.
- backend/src/routes/maintainer.ts: POST /api/maintainer/sling with body { kind, number, html_url, intent: 'review' | 'draft' | 'triage', target?: string }. Constructs the text from a per-intent template:
    review: 'Please review PR <html_url>'
    draft:  'Please draft a PR addressing <html_url>'
    triage: 'Please triage <html_url>'
  Falls through to MAINTAINER_SLING_TARGET env or 'mayor' when target is omitted. Audit-logged via recordAudit. Returns { ok: true, bead_id?, stdout } on success.
- backend/src/config.ts: maintainerSlingTarget config field (env MAINTAINER_SLING_TARGET, default 'mayor').

ACCEPTANCE
- Curl POST /api/maintainer/sling with CSRF + valid body dispatches a sling and audit-logs it.
- Invalid target / oversized text / unknown intent return 4xx with structured ApiError.
- gc sling exit non-zero surfaces as 502 with stderr in details.
notes:
PLAN: 

Goal: Add POST /api/maintainer/sling backend endpoint that dispatches 'gc sling <target> <text>' so the next bead (8dp per-row sling buttons) has a wire to call. No frontend in this bead.

Approach:
1. backend/src/exec.ts: add execGcSling(target, beadText). AGENT_ALIAS_RE on target, 4KB cap on beadText, 15s timeout, shell:false, clean env. Returns ExecResult; caller parses bead id from stdout best-effort.
2. backend/src/config.ts: add maintainerSlingTarget field (env MAINTAINER_SLING_TARGET, default 'mayor'). Validate against AGENT_ALIAS_RE at config load.
3. backend/src/routes/maintainer.ts: add POST /sling. Body { kind, number, html_url, intent, target? }. Per-intent templates render the bead text:
   - review: 'Please review PR <html_url>'
   - draft:  'Please draft a PR addressing <html_url>'
   - triage: 'Please triage <html_url>'
   Audit-logged via recordAudit. Returns { ok: true, bead_id?, stdout_excerpt } on success; 4xx validation; 502 for non-zero gc exit.
4. backend/src/server.ts: thread config.maintainerSlingTarget into maintainerRouter.
5. Dependency-inject the exec fn into maintainerRouter options (mirrors healthRouter pattern) so tests can stub without module mocking.

Files: backend/src/exec.ts, backend/src/config.ts, backend/src/routes/maintainer.ts, backend/src/server.ts, backend/test/maintainer-sling.test.ts (new).

Tests:
- Happy path: valid body -> 200, sling fn called with composed text + resolved target, audit row written.
- Default target fallback: target omitted -> resolves to maintainerSlingTarget config value.
- Invalid intent / invalid target alias / invalid kind / oversized html_url / non-positive number -> 400 validation.
- gc exit non-zero -> 502 with stderr detail.
- Audit row records { kind, number, intent, target, text_len } only — NEVER the full text (events.jsonl noise control).

Risks:
- v1 sling fires fire-and-forget but we still await the gc exit so we can surface failure synchronously. If gc supervisor is slow this blocks the request up to 15s. Acceptable for v1 (UX: 'slinging...' spinner). Async detach is a v2 concern.
- Validating MAINTAINER_SLING_TARGET env at config load means a bad env value fails server startup. That's the right call — better than a 500 per-request.
- gc sling may not be present on operator host. We don't probe at startup; 502+stderr is the runtime contract.

Out of scope:
- Frontend sling button (8dp)
- Triaged-gating of the endpoint (frontend-side concern; backend allows sling on any item)
- --formula routing / per-intent agent selection
- Async return path / bead-id polling (are bead)
PLAN-REVIEW (architect):

Six changes folded into the plan:

1. (H1) DI of execGcSling is a NEW pattern for write-exec routers, NOT 'mirrors healthRouter'. healthRouter DI is config-knob-only. Closest behavioural precedent is mailSendRouter (which doesn't DI). We're establishing the pattern; file follow-up bead to retrofit mailSendRouter later (out of scope).

2. (H2) Add 'dashboard.sling' to AdminAuditEvent.type explicit alternates in shared/src/index.ts:537. Don't fall through to | string — keep the type discriminator queryable for forensic review. Sling is semantically alongside dashboard.send_mail, not dashboard.exec.

3. (H3) MAINTAINER_SLING_TARGET validation: silent fall-back with console.warn, NOT crash startup. Matches parseIntervalMs precedent at config.ts:91-96. Reason: a typo in one optional env should not dark all five dashboard views. execGcSling re-validates target at request time so a bad config still surfaces as 400, not 500. AGENT_ALIAS_RE stays exec.ts-private (config no longer validates).

4. (M2) Audit row records BOTH parsed_args { kind, number, intent, target, text_len } AND top-level exit_code + duration_ms (mirrors mail-send.ts:44-52).

5. (M3) Test harness: new file backend/test/maintainer-sling.test.ts with its OWN app skeleton — buildApp() from routes.test.ts uses fake-HTTP-supervisor which doesn't help here. Stub execGcSling via DI; use setAuditLogPath + tmp file to assert audit write happens (exercises more of the real path than mocking recordAudit).

6. (L2) Bead-id stdout parsing lives in the ROUTE (mirrors mail-send.ts:62 message-id extraction), not in execGcSling. execGcSling returns plain ExecResult.

Also: kind stays in the request body even though intent already drives the template. Rationale: kind ('pr'|'issue') is audit-logged independently of intent and future template revisions may want 'Please triage PR <url>' vs 'Please triage issue <url>'. v1 templates are intent-only; kind is forward-compat. Plan now says so explicitly.

Plan is implementation-ready with these six revisions.
COORDINATION: P2 6fp (graph renderer + filter system + sling dropdown) drafted in same session. 6fp depends on ib5 sling exec+route foundation. Forward-compat wrinkle: 6fp wants formula-driven sling ('bd formula list' dropdown), ib5 v1 ships text-only. Clean shape: ib5 lands execGcSling(target, beadText) as planned; 6fp adds execGcSlingFormula(target, formulaName, contextArgs) as a separate sibling wrapper rather than overloading. Both share AGENT_ALIAS_RE + the 'dashboard.sling' audit type. Dep edge added.
PROGRESS: 

Implementation green.

- backend/src/exec.ts: execGcSling(target, beadText) with AGENT_ALIAS_RE + 4KB cap + 15s timeout

title: feat(maintainer): triaged-state persistence + per-row toggle + untriaged visual
description:
Foundation for distinguishing items the maintainer has actually vetted from items only mechanically scored by the 7ts heuristic. Today every row shows a triage_score but the page makes no visual distinction between 'this number is your considered judgment' and 'this number is a guess from labels + diff size.' Closes that gap.

DESIGN CONTEXT
- triage_score (introduced in 7ts) is explicitly a HEURISTIC, not a vetted assessment. It combines severity (priority/p* tier) + simplicity (PR diff size, PR status, issue friction labels). It's a starting-point ranking, not 'the maintainer agrees with this priority.'
- Vetted state per item needs its own persistence layer — must survive the nightly worker (ar9) rewriting the cache. Separate JSON file at $HOME/.gascity-dashboard/maintainer-triage-state.json keyed by '<kind>:<number>'. fetchTriage reads it after composeEnvelope and applies triaged + triaged_at to each item.
- Visual signal must pass the Greyscale Test. Editorial answer: italic title + small '?' leading marker (fg-faint) for untriaged; vetted items render at normal weight with no extra marker. NOT a card tint, NOT a side-stripe, NOT a colored dot (the maroon mark already owns that channel for One Mark).

SCOPE
- shared/src/index.ts: add 'triaged: boolean' + 'triaged_at: IsoTimestamp | null' to TriageItem.
- backend/src/maintainer/triaged-state.ts: atomic JSON read/write keyed by '<kind>:<number>'.
- backend/src/maintainer/triage.ts: after composeEnvelope, splice triaged-state onto each item.
- backend/src/routes/maintainer.ts: POST /api/maintainer/triaged with body { items: [{kind, number}], triaged: boolean } — flips one or many items, writes the state file, fires notifyRefresh so any open tab repaints.
- frontend/src/api/client.ts: api.maintainerSetTriaged(items, triaged) wrapper.
- frontend/src/routes/Maintainer.tsx: italic title + '?' leading marker for triaged=false; per-row 'Mark triaged' affordance (single-click toggle, no confirmation, reversible).

ACCEPTANCE
- Untriaged rows visually distinct in greyscale.
- Mark-triaged action persists across nightly worker refresh (state survives cache rewrite).
- The maroon One Mark continues to apply correctly (the marked item itself can be untriaged — those are independent signals).
notes:
PLAN:
GOAL
Distinguish maintainer-vetted items from heuristically-scored items, persisted in a state file that survives the nightly worker's cache rewrite. Untriaged rows render italic with a leading '?' marker; vetted rows are normal.

APPROACH
1. Extend shared TriageItem with triaged + triaged_at fields (default false / null).
2. New backend module backend/src/maintainer/triaged-state.ts (atomic JSON, kind:number keys, mirror storage.ts patterns).
3. composeEnvelope in triage.ts splices triaged-state onto each item AFTER tier classification, BEFORE building tier sections. Prune state entries no longer in envelope on every refresh (bounded growth).
4. POST /api/maintainer/triaged on maintainerRouter: body { items: [{kind, number}], triaged: boolean }. Validates, atomically updates state file, fires notifyRefresh, audit-logs.
5. Wire state path through AdminConfig + router options (DI consistent with ib5 execGcSling pattern). Tests inject a tmp path.
6. Frontend: api.maintainerSetTriaged(items, triaged) + italic title + leading '?' (text-fg-faint) for triaged=false + per-row 'Mark triaged' typographic button.

FILES TO TOUCH
- shared/src/index.ts (TriageItem fields)
- backend/src/maintainer/triaged-state.ts (NEW)
- backend/src/maintainer/triage.ts (load+splice state in composeEnvelope; takes statePath param)
- backend/src/maintainer/worker.ts (pass statePath through to fetchTriage)
- backend/src/routes/maintainer.ts (new POST /triaged; thread statePath through router options)
- backend/src/config.ts (new maintainerTriagedStatePath field + MAINTAINER_TRIAGED_STATE_PATH env)
- backend/src/server.ts (pass statePath to router + worker)
- frontend/src/api/client.ts (maintainerSetTriaged wrapper)
- frontend/src/routes/Maintainer.tsx (italic+? for untriaged + Mark-triaged button)
- backend/test/maintainer-triaged-state.test.ts (NEW unit)
- backend/test/maintainer-triaged.test.ts (NEW route+persistence)

TESTS
- triaged-state unit: ENOENT returns empty map; writeState round-trips; tmp+rename atomicity; shape validation rejects garbage.
- triage envelope: items with state entries have triaged=true + triaged_at; missing entries have triaged=false + null.
- POST /triaged route: happy path flips one and many items; flip-back unsets; SSE event fires; audit row written; validation rejects bad kind/number/non-bool triaged; oversized arrays rejected.
- Pruning: state entries whose kind:number is no longer in envelope are dropped after next refresh.

RISKS
1. Visual collision: 'italic' on title vs existing 'italic' on tier='new' contributor byline. Same family/weight, different elements, low risk — confirm via snap.
2. State unbounded growth: pruning step in composeEnvelope mitigates.
3. notifyRefresh from POST /triaged sends only the envelope-changed event; frontend refetch /triage gets fresh envelope with state applied. No need to send state diff over SSE.

OUT OF SCOPE
- Bulk select (0nn), per-row sling (8dp), agent-vetted assessment (are)
- Multi-repo state files (v0 single repo)
PLAN-REVIEW (architect findings folded in):

REVISIONS
1. SPLICE POINT (C1+C2): Move triaged-state splice OUT of composeEnvelope. Keep triaged flags OUT of the cached envelope entirely. Apply state in:
   - GET /triage route handler (read cache, then read state, then splice and return)
   - POST /refresh response (same: build fresh envelope, write cache, then splice state, then return + notifyRefresh)
   This eliminates the race between POST /triaged and nightly worker writes; cache only contains heuristic-derived data. Drops the worker.ts edit.
2. VISUAL (H1+H2): Drop italic title. The leading '?' (text-fg-faint) is the only signal for untriaged. Suppress '?' when is_marked is true (the maroon dot already owns 'this needs attention' — One Mark Rule preserved). This avoids the italic-collision with tier='new' contributor byline.
3. RESPONSE SHAPE (H3): POST /triaged returns { ok: true, updated: [{kind, number}], count } not just boolean. Lets 0nn surface partial outcomes.
4. PRUNING (M1): DO NOT prune state on refresh. Bounded growth is acceptable (single entry per ever-vetted item); pruning loses vetting on close/reopen. Document in triaged-state.ts header.
5. CONCURRENT WRITES (M3): triaged-state.ts uses an in-process Promise-chain mutex (six lines) to serialize read-modify-write across simultaneous POSTs from two tabs.
6. AUDIT (M4): POST /triaged records type='dashboard.maintainer.triaged' with parsed_args { count, triaged }.
7. FORWARD-COMPAT (out-of-scope lock for 'are'): shared TriageItem comment locks triaged_at semantics to 'maintainer ack timestamp', NOT agent assessment time. 'are' adds its own triage_assessment field with its own vetted_at later.

- backend/src/maintainer/triaged-state.ts (NEW: tmp+rename + in-process mutex)

ADDITIONAL TEST
- Concurrent POSTs to /triaged from two clients (Promise.all) end up with both items toggled, no lost update.

VERIFICATION CHECKLIST (M2)
- After implementation: open two tabs to /maintainer, toggle in tab A, confirm tab B repaints triaged state within ~1s via SSE refresh.
- Run scripts/snap.mjs maintainer in both themes; eyeball Greyscale Test on '?' + marked-row coexistence.

- Backend triaged-state.ts: tmp+rename atomic JSON, Promise-chain mutex, applyTriagedState splice helper. Path threaded via AdminConfig + MaintainerRouterOptions + MAINTAINER_TRIAGED_STATE_PATH env.
- Route: POST /api/maintainer/triaged with validation + audit ('dashboard.maintainer.triaged'). GET /triage and POST /refresh splice state at read time (cache file holds NO triaged data). notifyRefresh fires unconditionally, with synthetic envelope when cache absent.

title: feat(maintainer): distinguish issue vs PR rows + cross-link via body parse
description: The 361 ingest left two gaps the page exposes once live data lands: (1) standalone PR rows in unclustered render with pl-6 indent inherited from the 'PR under issue' layout, so an orphan PR reads as a dangling continuation of whatever issue happens to be above it; (2) no PR-to-issue linkage means the 'anchored' affordance never lights up. Fix both: re-add body to GH_PR_FIELDS (fits under MAX_BYTES_LARGE), parse PR bodies for (Fixes|Closes|Resolves) #N to populate pr.linked_numbers, then reverse-map to populate issue.linked_numbers with the PR numbers that fix them. Frontend: give each row a leading typographic kind marker ('PR' small-caps for PRs, blank for issues; maroon mark stays in same column on is_marked items) and stop indenting standalone PRs; only nest PRs when their parent issue is also visible in the current tier section.
close_reason: Two-part fix. Backend: re-added body to GH_PR_FIELDS, parse PR bodies with /\b(?:close[sd]?|fix(?:e[sd])?|resolve[sd]?)\s+#(\d+)\b/gi to populate pr.linked_numbers, then reverse-map to populate issue.linked_numbers with the PRs that fix them. Verified end-to-end: 20 of 143 PRs link to 19 unique open issues. Frontend: extracted RowList component that nests PRs under their parent issue when both are in the same view; introduced four greyscale-safe row states (regular issue / marked issue / standalone PR with 'PR' leading label / nested PR with '↳' glyph + pl-10 indent + muted title). 'anchored' label on issues now only lights up when the linked PR is OUT of view — when it's IN view, the visual nesting already says it.

title: feat(maintainer): priority classifier (labels to tier)
description: Deterministic classifier mapping GitHub labels to triage tier: REGRESSION+BREAKING, REGRESSION, STABILITY. close_reason: Priority classifier + triage scoring ships. Labels -> tier: kind/bug + priority/p0|p1 -> regression_breaking; kind/bug -> regression; everything else -> stability. Triage score combines severity_base (300/200/100 by tier) with a simplicity_bonus: PR diff size + review status for PRs; linked-PR presence + needs-info/needs-repro/stale penalties for issues. composeEnvelope sorts each tier section by score desc so the highest-priority-by-triage-skill items bubble to the top. One Mark Rule enforced: at most one maroon across the entire envelope. Mark candidates are non-blocked PRs in regression_breaking; the top scorer's mark transfers to its parent issue when the parent is in view, so the pair reads as one unit. Verified end-to-end: 47/129/228 tier split on gastownhall/gascity, exactly one mark (issue #2234, fix candidate PR #2462). title: Proxy SSE through backend /api/events/stream (decouple from direct supervisor reachability)
description:
Today the frontend opens EventSource directly against gc supervisor URL (http://127.0.0.1:8372/v0/city/{name}/events/stream) per architect addendum td-wisp-ijk7g. This breaks any deployment where the browser can't reach the supervisor's loopback bind — most commonly SSH-tunnel access where only :5174 is forwarded. The Agents page indicator shows 'offline' because EventSource onerror fires immediately.

Fix: pipe SSE through the backend at GET /api/events/stream. Backend opens an upstream connection to the supervisor, forwards Last-Event-ID, and streams events back to the client with the same event name ('event'). Frontend useGcEventRefresh switches to '/api/events/stream'. Drop supervisor URL from CSP connect-src. /api/config/gc-supervisor remains in place but the events hook no longer calls it.

Net effect: only :5174 (dev) or :8081 (prod) need to be reachable from the browser. SSH tunnel works with a single forward. Same-origin SSE also simplifies CSP.
notes:
PLAN: New backend route GET /api/events/stream opens upstream fetch to {supervisorUrl}/v0/city/{name}/events/stream, forwards Last-Event-ID header (or ?after= query) upstream, pipes the raw byte stream verbatim to the client (preserves SSE framing: 'event: event', 'data: ...', 'id: ...'). Mount OUTSIDE writeRouter so no CSRF requirement (EventSource can't carry a CSRF token). Cleanup: client disconnect aborts upstream. Heartbeat: send ':

' every 15s defensively. Frontend useGcEventRefresh drops the /api/config/gc-supervisor fetch and connects EventSource('/api/events/stream'). CSP: drop supervisorUrl from connect-src (now same-origin via 'self'). Files: NEW backend/src/routes/events.ts, NEW backend/test/events.test.ts, MOD backend/src/server.ts, MOD frontend/src/hooks/useGcEvents.ts, MOD backend/src/middleware/security.ts (drop stale comment). Tests: fake upstream SSE server, assert event byte-pipe + Last-Event-ID forward + disconnect cleanup. Risks: timeout middleware on long-lived response — confirm none; intermediaries dropping idle connections — heartbeat defends. Out of scope: removing /api/config/gc-supervisor (will become unused; flag for follow-up).
PLAN-REVIEW: Adversarial check — (a) Does forwarding Last-Event-ID header upstream actually resume correctly? gc supervisor accepts ?after= query per the current FE code; need to map Last-Event-ID header → ?after= upstream OR Last-Event-ID header upstream. Use the same pattern the FE uses today (?after=) for guaranteed parity. (b) Could the byte pipe corrupt SSE framing if a chunk boundary splits a frame? No — SSE is line-delimited and HTTP chunks are arbitrary; both sides parse line-by-line so chunk boundaries don't matter. (c) What if upstream returns non-200 immediately? Surface as a single SSE error event ('event: error
data: {...}

') then close — frontend will see this as a normal message and trigger reconnect via onerror eventually. Cleaner: forward upstream status to client as initial response status; the EventSource will treat non-200 as error. Pick the latter. (d) Does dropping the supervisor URL from CSP break anything else? Grep confirms /api/config/gc-supervisor is the ONLY consumer; safe to drop both the endpoint usage in the FE hook AND the CSP entry.
PROGRESS: implemented backend/src/routes/events.ts + tests (6 cases). Wired into server.ts at /api/events outside writeRouter. Removed /api/config/gc-supervisor endpoint (FE no longer needs it). Frontend useGcEvents now opens EventSource('/api/events/stream'). CSP connect-src reduced to 'self'. Backend 23/23 + frontend 42/42 tests pass. Live e2e against running gc supervisor: SSE headers correct, CSP no longer enumerates supervisor URL.
REVIEW: 3-reviewer Phase 4. All three converged on the SAME HIGH: drain await hangs forever if client disconnects during backpressure (orphan upstream connection + heartbeat timer). FIXED by racing drain against close event with shared resolver, plus a writableEnded check after. Other findings (cast removal in test, trailing-slash doc on supervisorUrl, redundant try/catch around cancel().catch(), heartbeat rationale comment) all addressed inline. Skipped: backpressure test (would need a fake slow client + chunked upstream; lower ROI than implementation polish). MEDIUM (no upstream fetch header timeout) deferred — loopback risk is small.
close_reason: DONE: SSE proxy live at GET /api/events/stream. Backend opens upstream fetch to gc supervisor, byte-pipes the SSE stream, supports Last-Event-ID/?after= resume, aborts upstream on client disconnect with drain-race protection. /api/config/gc-supervisor removed. CSP connect-src now 'self'. 23/23 backend + 42/42 frontend tests pass. Merged on main at edec592. Operator action: hard-reload the dashboard in the browser to pick up the new useGcEvents.ts; only :5174 (or :8081 in prod) now needs to be reachable from the browser.

title: Stale-while-revalidate cache for cross-route page swaps
description: Each route (Agents, Beads, Mail, Activity, Health) does useState(empty) + useEffect(() => api.listX()) on mount, so navigating away and back is always a cold fetch — currently ~1.4s per supervisor call. Add a module-level Map cache in frontend/src/api/cache.ts and a useCachedFetch(key, fetcher) hook that (a) renders cached data immediately on mount if present, (b) always kicks off a background refresh, (c) updates state when fresh data arrives, (d) keeps showing cached data on fetch error. Refactor each route to use the hook. Cache key per endpoint+params (e.g. agents, beads:open, mail:inbox:stephanie). Mutations call cache.invalidate(prefix) so the next render fetches fresh. Result: first visit is normal speed; subsequent swaps are instant + refresh in background.
close_reason: DONE: Added frontend/src/api/cache.ts (module-level Map with get/set/invalidate/invalidateKey) and frontend/src/hooks/useCachedData.ts (stale-while-revalidate hook). Refactored Agents, Beads, Mail, Activity, Health to use it — second visit to any route renders cached data synchronously while a background fetch refreshes. Refresh-button + SSE refreshes still flow through hook.refresh() and rewrite the cache. Mutations in-route auto-refresh via existing refresh() calls; cache.invalidate(prefix) available if future cross-route writes need it.

title: CSRF self-heal: frontend retries once on 403 kind=csrf
description: Each backend boot generates a new in-process CSRF bootToken (backend/src/middleware/csrf.ts). tsx watch restarts the backend on every source-file edit, rotating the token; the browser still holds the previous cookie until the next GET returns Set-Cookie. Mutations (peek, mail send, bead actions) made in that window 403 with kind:csrf. Fix in frontend/src/api/client.ts: when a non-GET request returns 403 with kind:csrf, fetch GET /api/csrf to refresh the cookie, then replay the original request exactly once. Single retry, no recursion, no retry on any other error class.
close_reason: DONE: api/client.ts wraps request() and self-heals on 403 kind=csrf by fetching /api/csrf to refresh the cookie, then replaying the original request once. Single retry, no recursion. Verified semantics by reading csrf middleware — token rotates per bootToken (per process), so frontend retry after the cookie-refresh GET sees the new value.

title: Group + filter + search across Beads, Agents, Mail views
description: The three list-heavy views (Beads, Agents, Mail) all surface items from multiple projects mixed together, with no way to focus on one project at a time and no in-page search. As the queue grows this is the main friction point. Design: (1) collapse/expand by project — each project gets a section header with a count badge and a chevron disclosure; remembered per-view per-session in localStorage. (2) search — a single text input per view that filters the visible list (substring match across title + identifier + relevant fields like 'from' for mail, 'session id' for agents). (3) filter chips for the high-signal axes: status for beads (open/in_progress/blocked/closed), state for agents (running/idle/stopped), read-state for mail (unread/read). Respect existing per-view conventions: beads already groups by status implicitly via the table; agents shows session state via the row; mail uses the persistent 'Reading as' strip. Don't add color or new components if existing utility classes can handle it (Greyscale Test). Out of scope: full-text search across mail bodies (defer to a separate bead). Acceptance: on a screen showing 30+ items, the operator can find a specific item in under 5 seconds without keyboard navigation.

Compare against what tmux/mayor itself reports for the same session at the same moment. May be a unit mismatch (tokens vs %), a stale-cache issue, or a wrong divisor. Acceptance: context % in the dashboard matches what mayor/tmux reports for the same session, within rounding.

title: Investigate slow /api/sessions and /api/beads endpoints
description: Both endpoints intermittently time out (>10s). Surfaces as 'No sessions running' / 'Nothing on the queue right now' empty states in the dashboard even though the gc supervisor has real data. Likely a slow shell-out to gc CLI in the backend's gc-client.ts. Profile backend/src/routes/sessions.ts and backend/src/routes/beads.ts to find the hot path; consider caching, parallel fetch, or talking to the supervisor's HTTP API directly instead of shelling out.
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=a82e3ffff1ad0a3ed1c010303cf10bb587472128 on branch=worktree-agent-aa01f312a2a328f6e contains base=a82e3ffff1ad0a3ed1c010303cf10bb587472128
PLAN: Root cause is NOT shell-out — gc-client.ts already uses HTTP fetch. Real issue: GcClient.getJson() has NO default timeout. Node 20 fetch has no default timeout either. When the gc supervisor hangs or is slow on /sessions or /beads (beads is 1000-item window, sessions can be large), the backend request hangs indefinitely until the browser bails (>10s). Routes never pass an AbortSignal so they are effectively unbounded.

Approach: (1) Add default per-request timeout in GcClient.getJson via AbortSignal.timeout, configurable per call (default 5000ms — short enough to fail fast, long enough for the 1000-bead window). (2) Pile on a small in-memory TTL cache wrapper (e.g. 2s) for listSessions/listBeads so simultaneous tab loads / SSE refreshes collapse to one upstream call (in-flight de-dup). (3) Convert 502 hang into a 504-with-message so the empty-state path is reached quickly.

Files: backend/src/gc-client.ts (add timeout + accept default), backend/src/routes/sessions.ts (translate AbortError -> 504), backend/src/routes/beads.ts (same), backend/src/cache.ts (new — TTL+inflight memo, small). Tests: backend/test/gc-client.test.ts (timeout fires), backend/test/cache.test.ts (TTL + inflight dedup).

Risks: (a) 5s ceiling on listBeads(limit=1000) might be too tight when supervisor is under load — bump if profiling shows it. (b) Caching reads of dynamic state means an action-then-immediate-reload may see stale; 2s window is short enough that the SSE event-driven refresh still feels live. (c) Adding a tiny test runner increases dev-dep surface.

Out of scope: frontend changes, mail/git/builds/health, ring-buffer source, anything not on /api/sessions or /api/beads hot path.
PLAN-REVIEW: (a) Use Node 20 built-in node:test runner — zero new deps. (b) Drop TTL from MVP; in-flight request coalescing (single-flight per URL) is the higher-value, lower-staleness simplification. Two simultaneous /api/sessions calls collapse to one upstream call. (c) Translate AbortError -> 504 'upstream timeout' so the user sees a fast empty state, not a 10s+ hang. (d) Keep default timeout configurable via env (DEFAULT_GC_TIMEOUT_MS, default 5000) so it can be tuned without code change. (e) Minimum diff: gc-client.ts (timeout + coalescing wrapper), routes/sessions.ts (504 mapping), routes/beads.ts (504 mapping), plus tests in backend/test/. No route signature changes.
PROGRESS: Implemented (1) default 5s timeout in GcClient via AbortSignal.timeout (configurable via GC_CLIENT_TIMEOUT_MS env or opts.defaultTimeoutMs); (2) in-flight request coalescing — concurrent identical GETs share a single upstream call; per-caller signal is composed at await-layer so one caller's abort doesn't kill a coalesced fetch; (3) GcClient.isTimeoutError static for clean error classification; (4) sessions.ts + beads.ts map timeout -> 504 'upstream-timeout', non-timeout failure stays 502 'upstream'. Tests: 11 passing in backend/test/{gc-client,routes}.test.ts using node:test (no new deps) + tsx via 'npm run test'. Both typecheck targets pass.

Frontend typecheck has pre-existing baseline failures (vitest not installed, missing shared export effectiveContextPct) — verified out of scope by stash-and-recheck.
REVIEW: 3 reviewers ran in parallel. code-reviewer + ts-reviewer both flagged isTimeoutError over-broad (AbortError arm conflated caller-abort with timeout). Fixed in-wave: narrowed to TimeoutError only + updated caller-signal test to positively assert AbortError + !isTimeoutError. Filed gascity-dashboard-mek (extend 504 mapping to mail.ts/health.ts) and gascity-dashboard-ais (strip upstream URL from fetchOnce error). 11 backend tests + both backend typechecks green on merged review branch.
close_reason: DONE: GcClient hot-path bounded. (1) Default per-request timeout via AbortSignal.timeout (env GC_CLIENT_TIMEOUT_MS, default 5000ms). (2) In-flight request coalescing per URL — concurrent identical GETs share one upstream call; caller signals compose at await layer so one abort doesn't kill the shared fetch. (3) Routes map timeout to HTTP 504 upstream-timeout; non-timeout failures stay 502. Hypothesis in description (slow shell-out) was wrong — gc-client already used HTTP; root cause was no timeout. 11 node:test tests, no new deps. Phase 4 narrowed isTimeoutError to TimeoutError only. Merged via wave-kz8-9yb-review at 15ded785e58857f3ce166e2eefd84ab6c7040cf0.

title: feat(maintainer): post-sling success toast with link to agent session
description:
Surfaced from gascity-dashboard-f0e session. After a successful sling (single or bulk), the user needs feedback that the dispatch landed AND a quick way to follow up on the agent that's now working it. Today the only feedback is the action bar disappearing — silent on success.

SCOPE
- frontend/src/routes/Maintainer.tsx: add slingSuccess state alongside the existing slingError. On dispatchSlings success path (line ~178), set { count, target } and auto-clear after ~5s via setTimeout.
- Render the success line in the same bottom-bar region as slingError. Copy: 'Slung N to <target>. View in Agents →' with the trailing arrow as a Link to /agents (or /agents?alias=<target> if the route supports a query filter — check first).
- DESIGN.md: same typographic register as the existing slingError line. No card chrome, no toast library, no animation chrome. Single hairline above the bottom region or just space-separation.
- Single-item slings (per-row buttons from ib5) get the same treatment — same slingSuccess state, same component path. Today those slings hit /api/maintainer/sling directly and have no user-visible confirmation either.
- Test (vitest): assert slingSuccess sets on dispatchSlings success, clears after timeout, and renders the agent link.

OUT OF SCOPE
- WorkflowMap (bead 0t6) — phase-lane progression view is the deeper feedback surface; this toast is the immediate 'it landed' signal.
- Per-bead-id linking — wds (P3) blocks getting the real bead_id back from sling. Until wds lands, toast can only link to the agent, not the specific bead. Fine for v1.
- Persistent activity log of recent slings (better fit for /activity view or a future maintainer-history bead).

ACCEPTANCE
- After successful bulk sling: success line appears below the data, contains 'Slung N to <target>' + a working link to /agents.
- Line auto-clears after 5s OR on next action (whichever first).
- No regression in slingError display path.
- Vitest covers the success-state lifecycle.

title: fix(maintainer): bead_id regex doesn't match modern 'Created gc-NNN —' stdout
description:
Surfaced when fixing f0e. Modern 'gc sling' stdout format is:

  Created gc-255139 — "..."
  Auto-convoy gc-255141
  Attached wisp gc-255140 (formula "mol-focus-review") to gc-255139
  Slung gc-255139 (with default formula "mol-focus-review") → oversight-rig.chief-of-staff

The wave-8nj fix at backend/src/routes/maintainer.ts:20 set:
  const BEAD_ID_RE = /created bead ([A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9_.-]{0,63})/

But 'gc sling' emits 'Created <id>' (capital C, no 'bead'), not 'created bead <id>'. Result: bead_id is omitted from sling responses again (silent — matches the LOW-severity behavior the wave was trying to fix).

FIX: anchor on the 'Slung <id> ...' line (more specific than 'Created' which appears 3+ times in the output for multi-bead workflows). Regex like:
  const BEAD_ID_RE = /^Slung ([A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9_.-]{0,63})\b/m

Update the test fixture to match the modern multi-line output.
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=b4da6cae79c9e1209a7eeb4fdc7111d346c8f30d on branch=worktree-agent-aa343df734b6b1245 contains base=b4da6cae79c9e1209a7eeb4fdc7111d346c8f30d
PLAN: Goal — anchor bead_id extraction on the modern 'Slung <id>' line emitted by gc sling so /api/maintainer/sling responses carry the right id again. Approach — replace BEAD_ID_RE in backend/src/routes/maintainer.ts with /^Slung ([A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9_.-]{0,63})\b/m and update the comment. Files: backend/src/routes/maintainer.ts, backend/test/maintainer-sling.test.ts. Tests: (1) modern multi-line fixture per description, assert bead_id=gc-255139; (2) negative — stdout with no 'Slung' line returns bead_id=undefined; (3) refresh the existing happy-path & 'modern supervisor bead-id' fixtures to use the Slung-line stdout shape so they don't drift. Risks — there's also an existing test fixture that uses the legacy 'created bead <id>' shape; with the anchored regex those will RED unless we update them too. That's correct behaviour: the legacy shape is gone from gc. Out of scope — exec.ts BEAD_ID_RE (validator alphabet, unrelated).
PLAN-REVIEW: Adversarial pass on /^Slung ([A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9_.-]{0,63})\b/m. (a) Over-match — could a city/agent name contain 'Slung' on its own line? Looking at gc stdout from description, 'Slung <id> (with default formula ...) → <target>' is the canonical final-line shape; the bead id alphabet (no spaces, no parens) and the \b boundary stop at the space before '(with...)'. Even if a target alias contained 'Slung' substring, the ^ anchor + multiline flag scopes to line start, and AGENT_ALIAS_RE already constrains target shape. (b) Multi-target sling — gc sling currently emits exactly one Slung line per invocation (the trailing 'Slung <id> ... → target' summary). If gc later grows multi-target dispatch with multiple Slung lines, /m+exec returns the FIRST match which is acceptable & deterministic. (c) Quoted bead text containing 'Slung' substring — bead text appears in the 'Created <id> — "<text>"' line, never at line-start of a separate line, so ^ anchor protects against that. (d) Lookalike chars — the character class is identical to wave-8nj's; no regression on id alphabet. Conclusion: regex is tight enough.
PROGRESS: BEAD_ID_RE swapped to /^Slung ([A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9_.-]{0,63})\b/m in backend/src/routes/maintainer.ts:23. Test fixtures in backend/test/maintainer-sling.test.ts updated: defaultStub stdout switched to the modern multi-line envelope (Created/Auto-convoy/Attached wisp/Slung), happy-path assertion now expects bead_id=gc-255139, the previous 'modern supervisor bead-id' test rewritten for the Slung-line shape, and a NEW negative test added asserting bead_id=undefined when stdout has Created/Attached lines but no Slung line. Test results: backend test suite — 127/127 pass (was 125/127 pre-fix; the 2 reds were the new wds test + the happy-path with the new multi-line stub). Typecheck + typecheck:test both clean.

title: design(maintainer): selection checkbox + ● mark co-occurrence violates One Mark Rule
description:
Phase 4 code-reviewer MEDIUM finding from wave-8nj.

When the operator enters bulk-selection mode in /maintainer, the native checkbox renders with accent-accent (maroon tick). If the same row also has item.is_marked=true, the row shows two maroon affordances: the checkbox tick AND the ● mark in column 2. DESIGN.md One Mark Rule says 'maroon appears at most once per visible viewport' — violated.

Beads.tsx uses the same accent-accent class but does not co-occur with the ● mark, so it's not affected.

Two fix options:
1. Suppress the ● mark on selected rows (checkbox already signals attention)
2. Use a non-maroon accent for the checkbox in /maintainer (e.g., accent-fg)

File: frontend/src/routes/Maintainer.tsx lines ~604-609 (IssueRow + PrRow). Verify with node scripts/snap.mjs maintainer once the fix lands.
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=b4da6cae79c9e1209a7eeb4fdc7111d346c8f30d on branch=worktree-agent-afe7775cffe4cd03e contains base=b4da6cae79c9e1209a7eeb4fdc7111d346c8f30d
PLAN: Option 2 — swap accent-accent → accent-fg on SelectCheckbox (Maintainer.tsx:571). Single shared component covers both IssueRow + PrRow checkboxes. Tailwind config exposes 'fg' color (light=18% 0.012 75 / dark=92% 0.006 75), so accent-fg resolves to accent-color: oklch(var(--fg)). High contrast against surface in both themes. Preserves ● as the persistent triage signal; checkbox switches to warm-neutral. Beads.tsx accent-accent left alone — no ● co-occurrence there. No select-all header checkbox in Maintainer.tsx, only the per-row instance. Verify with snap.mjs maintainer.
PLAN-REVIEW: (a) accent-fg contrast verified — fg token is 18%/92% L (light/dark) vs surface 96%/16%, very strong contrast; (b) PriorityBadge also uses text-accent for high-pri items — separate pre-existing One Mark concern, out of scope for this bead which is specifically checkbox+●; (c) focus-mark ring is maroon but transient (keyboard focus only), not a persistent co-occurrence; (d) no select-all header checkbox to fix; (e) no row hover maroon-state to fix.
